Even during times when the gospel was first spreading, there would be dangerous times; because of persecution from outside, but even more so because of corruption within. People love to satisfy their own desires more than to please God and do what is right. When everyone is eager for what they can get, and anxious to keep what they have, this makes people dangerous to one another. When people do not fear God, they will not respect others. When children are disobedient to their parents, that makes the times perilous. People are unholy and without the fear of God because they are unthankful for the mercies of God. We abuse God’s gifts if we use them to satisfy our desires. Times are also perilous when parents have no natural affection for their children. And when people do not control their own spirits, but despise what is good and honorable. God is to be loved above all; but a worldly mind, full of hostility toward him, prefers anything before him, especially worldly pleasure. A form of godliness is very different from true power; real Christians must withdraw from those who are found to be hypocrites. Such people have been found within the outward church, in every place, and at all times. There always have been skillful people, who, by pretense and flattery, creep into the favor and confidence of those who are too easily convinced, ignorant, and fanciful. Everyone must continually learn to know the Lord; but these people follow every new idea, yet never seek the truth as it is in Jesus. Like the Egyptian magicians, these were people with corrupt minds, prejudiced against the truth, and found to be completely without faith. Yet though the spirit of error may be released for a time, Satan can deceive the nations and the churches no further, and no longer, than God will permit.

The more fully we know the doctrine of Christ, as taught by the apostles, the closer we shall cling to it. When we know only part of the afflictions of believers, they tempt us to abandon the cause for which they suffer. A form of godliness, a profession of Christian faith without a godly life, often is allowed to pass, while an open profession of the truth as it is in Jesus, and resolute attention to the duties of godliness, stir up the scorn and enmity of the world. As good people, by the grace of God, grow better, so bad people, through the craft of Satan, and the power of their own corruptions, grow worse. The way of sin is downhill; such people go on from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived. Those who deceive others, deceive themselves, as they will find at last, to their cost. The history of the outward church terribly shows that the apostle spoke this as he was moved by the Holy Spirit.

Those who want to learn about God’s things, and be assured of them, must know the Holy Scriptures, for they are the Divine revelation. Childhood is the age to learn; and those who want true learning, must get it from the Scriptures. They must not lie neglected, seldom or never looked into. The Bible is a sure guide to eternal life. The prophets and apostles did not speak from themselves, but delivered what they received of God. It is profitable for all purposes of the Christian life. It is useful to everyone, because everyone needs to be taught, corrected, and reproved. There is something in the Scriptures suitable for every case. Oh that we may love our Bibles more, and keep closer to them! then shall we find benefit, and at last gain the happiness therein promised by faith in our Lord Jesus Christ, who is the main subject of both Testaments. We best oppose error by promoting a solid knowledge of the word of truth; and the greatest kindness we can do to children, is to make them early to know the Bible.
